_comment: Reference copy of the autoscaler policy + the fixture the Pester tests read for thresholds. The runbook (runbook/Invoke-CapacityAutoscale.ps1) carries its own embedded copy that actually runs - edit the $EmbeddedConfigJson block there for live changes. Thresholds are documentation-grounded (see docs/scaling-policy.md): scale up on sustained >80% or any throttling; scale down only when sustained <30% AND the smaller SKU stays under targetHeadroomPct.

evaluation._comment: consecutiveSignalsRequired = how many recent hourly snapshots must all agree before acting (hysteresis). Scale-down demands more confirmations than scale-up. cooldownMinutes = min time since the last resize before another is allowed.

notifications._comment: webhookUrl = a Teams Incoming Webhook / Power Automate Workflows URL. Empty = notifications off. notifyOnDryRun also covers cooldown-skipped actions.

boundaries._comment: Resizes that cross these boundaries are slower / have licensing side effects (per Microsoft docs). The runbook still performs them but logs a warning and treats them conservatively.

azure._comment: resourceGroup is per-capacity below because capacities can live in different RGs/regions.

defaults._comment: reservedFloorSku: scaling below a reserved instance does not reduce the bill (docs), so never auto-scale below it. null = no reservation (PAYG), free to shrink to minSku.

tmcadlfabric._comment: Set reservedFloorSku to your reserved size (e.g. F64) if this capacity is on an Azure reservation; leave null if pay-as-you-go.
